name: TitleMax Title Pawns
description_short: TitleMax offers short-term title loans and title pawns secured by vehicle titles, with no credit check required. Funding in as little as 30 minutes at 900+ US locations.
description_long:
TitleMax opened its first store on September 1, 1998, in Columbus, Georgia, and has grown into one of the largest title lending operations in the United States under the TMX Finance Family of Companies — which also operates TitleBucks and InstaLoan. Headquartered at 15 Bull Street in Savannah, Georgia, the company runs 900+ locations across 14 states and employs more than 2,000 people. TitleMax holds no CDFI, HUD, or NFCC certifications and is not BBB accredited. Its regulatory record includes two significant CFPB enforcement actions totaling $24M in penalties.

TitleMax's core product is the title loan or title pawn — a short-term, high-cost loan secured by the borrower's vehicle title. The borrower surrenders the title (not the car), continues driving, and repays within the contract period. Loan amounts range from a few hundred dollars up to $25,000 depending on vehicle value and applicable state law; Mississippi caps loans at $3,250 and Tennessee at $6,500. APRs are extremely high — typically 119% to 179% annually on a contract basis, and up to 25% per month (300%+ APR equivalent) under Georgia's title pawn statute. In select states, TitleMax also offers unsecured personal loans. Origination is conducted in-store in most states; no credit check is required, and funding can happen in as little as 30 minutes.

TitleMax's primary selling point is speed and accessibility: walk in with a qualifying vehicle title and valid ID, and most applicants can leave with cash within the hour regardless of credit history. With no prepayment penalties and a broad in-store network across 14 states, it operates in markets that conventional lenders largely ignore. A mobile app (iOS and Android) and online portal at account.titlemax.com let borrowers check balances, view due dates, make payments, and review payment history without visiting a branch.

The honest picture is sobering. TitleMax's interest costs rank among the highest in consumer finance — Georgia's title pawn structure charges 30 days of interest upfront as the first payment, and repeated rollovers compound costs dramatically. The company has been penalized twice by the CFPB: a $9M fine in 2016 for deceptive sales tactics and improper consumer data exposure, and a $15M settlement in 2023 for violating the Military Lending Act by charging active-duty servicemembers above the 36% MAPR cap, inserting illegal arbitration clauses, and failing required disclosures. State-level fines have been assessed in California ($25K), Pennsylvania ($1.6M restitution), and Illinois ($527K+). ComplaintsBoard shows a 2.0/5 rating from 87 complaints covering repossession disputes and loan processing failures. TitleMax is a last-resort option for vehicle owners with no other access to emergency cash — but the cost is extremely high and vehicle repossession is a real risk if repayment falters.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee offered. This is a lending product, not a subscription service.

tiers.name: Title Loan / Title Pawn
features: Loan amounts from a few hundred dollars up to $25,000 based on vehicle value
APR typically 119%–179%+ annually; up to 300%+ APR under title pawn regulations
No credit check required — bad credit and no credit accepted
Funding in as little as 30 minutes in-store
Borrower keeps driving vehicle during loan term
First payment structured as 30 days of interest

pros: No credit check required — borrowers with poor or no credit history are accepted
Funding available in as little as 30 minutes in-store
Borrower keeps driving their vehicle throughout the loan term
900+ locations across 14 states — one of the largest title lending networks in the US
Founded September 1, 1998 — over 27 years of title lending operations
No prepayment penalties — loans can be paid off early without fees
Mobile app (iOS and Android) plus online portal for payment and account management
cons: Extremely high cost — APRs of 119%–179%+ annually, up to 300%+ APR under Georgia title pawn law
CFPB fined TMX Finance $15M in 2023 for violating the Military Lending Act, illegal arbitration clauses, and charging servicemembers above the 36% MAPR cap
Not BBB accredited; ComplaintsBoard shows 2.0/5 from 87 complaints citing repossession disputes and loan processing failures
Vehicle repossession risk — the title is held as collateral and the car can be seized if the borrower defaults
In-store origination required in most states — no fully online application process
best_for: Vehicle owners with poor or no credit history who need fast emergency cash
Borrowers who cannot qualify for traditional bank loans, credit union loans, or personal loans
People who own their vehicle outright or have significant equity and need same-day funds
Short-term borrowers confident they can repay quickly to minimize extremely high interest costs
diagnosis: TitleMax is genuinely best suited for vehicle owners with bad or no credit who have exhausted other borrowing options and need cash the same day. The critical caveat is cost and risk: APRs of 119%–300%+ make this one of the most expensive forms of credit available, and a documented pattern of regulatory violations — including a $15M CFPB penalty for predatory practices against military servicemembers — warrants serious caution before borrowing.
services: Car title loans (secured by vehicle title)
Online account portal (account.titlemax.com) — balance, payments, history
Mobile app for iOS and Android — account management
In-store customer service at 900+ locations across 14 states
